name: Voter Action Project Research Survey
subject: Tracking poll: Term limits for the Supreme Court?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Voter Action Project
BCJ: Why Term Limits for Supreme Court Justices Make Sense [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA007/1/89215/images/BrennanCenter_SCOTUS_TermLimits.png]
Judicial historians and other nonpartisan justice activists are restarting their campaigns for term limits on Supreme Court justices in the wake of the presidential election. They argue that lifetime appointments are misplaced in the common era and that a rotation of federal justices off the Supreme Court and back to the Court of Appeals would bring a larger sense of fairness to the proceedings in front of our highest court.
We want to hear from voters in your area to get their feelings on the topic. Will you take our one-question poll right away?
Team: Should Supreme Court justices be term-limited?
By clicking a response below your answer will be automatically recorded:
YES [https://secure.everyaction.com/WI7XQsH6D0Kn6HzC8Tsadw2?am=15.00&contactdata=PAJqAlJ9Xm+ea/vCZmd5eKwJiTpRkDoM/3EkUZRID7LPwz0HVDsILTorF97Tq0qD+r1/rNfRM5Cg1wh5SydQomyU2/DjrYE8DjnfB7ORxzk=] NO [https://secure.everyaction.com/WI7XQsH6D0Kn6HzC8Tsadw2?am=15.00&contactdata=PAJqAlJ9Xm+ea/vCZmd5eKwJiTpRkDoM/3EkUZRID7LPwz0HVDsILTorF97Tq0qD+r1/rNfRM5Cg1wh5SydQomyU2/DjrYE8DjnfB7ORxzk=]

Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.everyaction.com/Qyv_-_BiF0iduOUt5sZ0yw2?am=20.00&contactdata=PAJqAlJ9Xm+ea/vCZmd5eKwJiTpRkDoM/3EkUZRID7LPwz0HVDsILTorF97Tq0qD+r1/rNfRM5Cg1wh5SydQomyU2/DjrYE8DjnfB7ORxzk=]

name: Royce White
subject: The Libs are like screaming kids at Target
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ROYCE WHITE FOR SENATE
body:
Democracy has become a dog whistle for globalism | [ ](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=ce1ce9e616484205a28a45cfaf96cecf&_m=c25702ebc8534cc895ccbc77260c1d7f&_e=4rVnp3oC0XEodw3FLNy7RzsLaEEiOpyL7iL9ygTf2CUW_EQmKJQJoxmy4ZMp77HIzLJ73GETVjq08PPkMVjgBo_7_QEG1GpGZdw9VX3yVonVFovzLl7f4GOBAoQwqqRGpHBO6XJCvLdEFcLD_6Dga-ZTgnQF4uhT2eeYxOE6H8fGzzUCxoixFkTL5pag4YL24HiJeuyz7geSx6pQAYp298UcabfryYB8cvKuUrL0syrOMT7lwo3A6T1FitWcUqxvgQT9dLeHZuj5Am7olSILuw2ZeXycyikIBWfuk1tPhpnuxTEoSqKDJnoBkBAy9RQyR9UFEzRdHBtOQ60H6hRf4w%3D%3D)
---
Peter,

_The Libs are just kicking and screaming like kids who didn’t get the toy they wanted at Target._

**They took their shot. They missed.** Now he’s here, and the Golden Age is happening. I LOVED EVERY MINUTE OF IT!

[](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=ce1ce9e616484205a28a45cfaf96cecf&_m=c25702ebc8534cc895ccbc77260c1d7f&_e=4rVnp3oC0XEodw3FLNy7RzsLaEEiOpyL7iL9ygTf2CUW_EQmKJQJoxmy4ZMp77HIzLJ73GETVjq08PPkMVjgBo_7_QEG1GpGZdw9VX3yVonVFovzLl7f4GOBAoQwqqRGStpegK0Rdo2u9u3U4LEC3FY5p0HPmciUMsac--oTNYyl8wX0ygzoF0wqG7ETQBF8M0NxwD4bA6VJ0z7WDhtBbUU3qEdTB2bVlHmrIHKr9NGaBUb-wXFfO8_d6KyIyc_pHVIMbM6Dje8EXm0DJILoduYxG8Mfgsl-DeN4fTyqtEhR5JQgDF2YvdP1Lyvy51ziDTHzUswNVHI_5RRSmRGVwg%3D%3D)
One of my favorite things about President Trump’s second term is the DISMANTLING of the post-World War II Democrat Liberal Order. I’ve been hammering this for over two years now. The international new world order has FAILED. It’s been a hindrance on Western Civilization, especially the United States seeing as our taxpayers are fronting the bill for it.

**“Democracy” has become a dog whistle for globalism.** When they say “democracy,” they don’t mean by the people, for the people. They mean the democratic institutions that sprung out of World War II, which are the load-bearing walls for their own globalist agenda.

**Anyone who threatens to tear down those load-bearing walls is considered an enemy. That’s me, Donald Trump, and our America-First Movement.**

Americans had to decide if they wanted to live in a 1945 America or a 1776 America. Me, personally? I want to live in a 1776 America where our “Democracy” is FOR THE PEOPLE AND BY THE PEOPLE! Based on last November, it seems like the American People are with ME.

**The Left and the Globalists have entrapped us in a HUGE PONZY SCHEME – and the American people are paying for it.**

They have this weird idea that revolution is just a bunch of drama. That’s just what we saw with their dramatic, theatrical, and shameful behavior at Trump’s Joint Address to Congress. Al Green made a fool of himself and got kicked out. They all held up stupid signs, and I didn’t see ONE Democrat stand and applaud for Trump’s honored guests, including a brain cancer surviving 13-year-old who was appointed into the Secret Service.

**If you want to talk about real revolution, it’s exactly what Donald Trump is doing.** He’s CHANGING THE BROKEN SYSTEM. You see some of the most Radical Leftists touting for revolution, like my former opponent Ilhan Omar. They’ve done nothing to change the system, in fact, they’ve been grifting off the system.

_They can kick and scream like children all they want. In fact, I want them to. It only opens the door for us to elect more America-First Republicans like me._

I know this was long-winded, but it’s important to blow the whistle when I see a foul.

[**If you agree with anything that I’ve said, I need your help. Can you chip in $15 before my midnight deadline to help me beat the Radical Left in my Senate campaign?**](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=ce1ce9e616484205a28a45cfaf96cecf&_m=c25702ebc8534cc895ccbc77260c1d7f&_e=4rVnp3oC0XEodw3FLNy7RzsLaEEiOpyL7iL9ygTf2CUW_EQmKJQJoxmy4ZMp77HIzLJ73GETVjq08PPkMVjgBo_7_QEG1GpGZdw9VX3yVonVFovzLl7f4GOBAoQwqqRGpHBO6XJCvLdEFcLD_6Dga-ZTgnQF4uhT2eeYxOE6H8fGzzUCxoixFkTL5pag4YL2xtNq5PAcWnVBhoKAXIIOhmSBLifww08OxByzW8hJEg51lVxYvqF-aargwJiFhMfULpAQFqg0bb3TdFtglZlWQL4oAbUfH5E4YQdDVz_VbSFdl9WmxhrnAp9zuoXzO0V5T9bwsDMAZLFnTuV3wRwrrw%3D%3D)

I don’t know who I’ll be facing just yet. It’s bound to be one of Ilhan Omar, Keith Ellison, Tim Walz, Peggy Flanagan, or Angie Craig. I don’t care who I face. Each of them is more Radical than the last. The People are Coming!

[**Can you rush a donation before midnight?**](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=ce1ce9e616484205a28a45cfaf96cecf&_m=c25702ebc8534cc895ccbc77260c1d7f&_e=4rVnp3oC0XEodw3FLNy7RzsLaEEiOpyL7iL9ygTf2CUW_EQmKJQJoxmy4ZMp77HIzLJ73GETVjq08PPkMVjgBo_7_QEG1GpGZdw9VX3yVonVFovzLl7f4GOBAoQwqqRGpHBO6XJCvLdEFcLD_6Dga-ZTgnQF4uhT2eeYxOE6H8fGzzUCxoixFkTL5pag4YL26AbPqVYxd3X1GymqJA3_WGSXePeIwZJKv-qYTM8yVRtUGhWA4gLFL0vm-UVYfVQ_839v9ZY-JUxRurigunucYcdTcCQH6N3nDknUv1WpVNM42h_biH0pfDMMzrlxB0fvtlNvbUvsismYHHpc2u0QAA%3D%3D)

Thank you,
Royce White

| [](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=ce1ce9e616484205a28a45cfaf96cecf&_m=c25702ebc8534cc895ccbc77260c1d7f&_e=4rVnp3oC0XEodw3FLNy7RzsLaEEiOpyL7iL9ygTf2CUW_EQmKJQJoxmy4ZMp77HIzLJ73GETVjq08PPkMVjgBo_7_QEG1GpGZdw9VX3yVonVFovzLl7f4GOBAoQwqqRGpHBO6XJCvLdEFcLD_6Dga-ZTgnQF4uhT2eeYxOE6H8fGzzUCxoixFkTL5pag4YL2SUwNE04uCJOflIGjEkHnt3h8xNvKxcNH6hAbN32TtYWOQ5Jxt-v48L9xuJFnxJhJ3QyWc0c9zDgy4vTLdOzfnVCpURXnuI_tNAeAt0uaNJHqucJfAexLtcGGKzLI48MR9-mKJ9h-t_XgLRpDgzPhHw%3D%3D)

Meet Royce White:

**Born and raised in the Twin Cities, Royce White was the son of a single mother and started playing sports at the age of 5 years old. Living in various neighborhoods growing up, much of his family remained in the Rondo community, where he spent much of his time and received schooling. White excelled at basketball and recieved a scholarship to play basketball at the University of Minnesota, and later transferred to Iowa State. He would go on to make Dean's List while receiving All Big-12 first-team honors and many other basketball awards.

_After being selected 16th overall by The Houston Rockets in the NBA draft, White began to voice concerns about the NBA’s collective bargaining agreement (CBA), specifically regarding mental health policy, which at the time did not exist. Eventually, his concerns became a public dispute between White and the NBA. This resulted in White demanding that the NBA, as a representative of the global corporate community, take up the responsibility of spearheading the mental health conversation – not just posturing through press statements, but through policy._

Royce then went on to win two championships in Canada before moving over to play Big 3 Basketball. White appeared at a game in a t-shirt that read “Free The Uyghurs.” After a game-winning basket, White did a post-game interview on CBS, where he called for 2 Million Uyghurs to be freed and released from concentration camps. As the season went on, White would touch on taboo topics such as Gain of Function Research, The Federal Reserve, Julian Assange, Edward Snowden, Larry Hoover, Jeff Fort, Muammar Gaddafi, The Great Reset, Ivermectin, Me Too, etc.

Now, Royce White is running for Senate in Minnesota to take down the Radical Left. Republicans have not seen recent success in Minnesota, but Royce's campaign is different: a grassroots movement that's sick of the Radical Agenda pushed by Far Left Democrats Tina Smith and Ilhan Omar. Royce needs your help to pull off a fourth quarter comeback victory and FLIP Minnesota Red for Republicans.

clean_body: Democracy has become a dog whistle for globalism | Peter, The Libs are just kicking and screaming like kids who didn’t get the toy they wanted at Target. They took their shot. They missed. Now he’s here, and the Golden Age is happening. I LOVED EVERY MINUTE OF IT! One of my favorite things about President Trump’s second term is the DISMANTLING of the post-World War II Democrat Liberal Order. I’ve been hammering this for over two years now. The international new world order has FAILED. It’s been a hindrance on Western Civilization, especially the United States seeing as our taxpayers are fronting the bill for it. “Democracy” has become a dog whistle for globalism. When they say “democracy,” they don’t mean by the people, for the people. They mean the democratic institutions that sprung out of World War II, which are the load-bearing walls for their own globalist agenda. Anyone who threatens to tear down those load-bearing walls is considered an enemy. That’s me, Donald Trump, and our America-First Movement. Americans had to decide if they wanted to live in a 1945 America or a 1776 America. Me, personally? I want to live in a 1776 America where our “Democracy” is FOR THE PEOPLE AND BY THE PEOPLE! Based on last November, it seems like the American People are with ME. The Left and the Globalists have entrapped us in a HUGE PONZY SCHEME – and the American people are paying for it. They have this weird idea that revolution is just a bunch of drama. That’s just what we saw with their dramatic, theatrical, and shameful behavior at Trump’s Joint Address to Congress. Al Green made a fool of himself and got kicked out. They all held up stupid signs, and I didn’t see ONE Democrat stand and applaud for Trump’s honored guests, including a brain cancer surviving 13-year-old who was appointed into the Secret Service. If you want to talk about real revolution, it’s exactly what Donald Trump is doing. He’s CHANGING THE BROKEN SYSTEM. You see some of the most Radical Leftists touting for revolution, like my former opponent Ilhan Omar. They’ve done nothing to change the system, in fact, they’ve been grifting off the system. They can kick and scream like children all they want. In fact, I want them to. It only opens the door for us to elect more America-First Republicans like me. I know this was long-winded, but it’s important to blow the whistle when I see a foul. I don’t know who I’ll be facing just yet. It’s bound to be one of Ilhan Omar, Keith Ellison, Tim Walz, Peggy Flanagan, or Angie Craig. I don’t care who I face. Each of them is more Radical than the last. The People are Coming! name: Katie Porter
subject: It’s just lip service
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Truth to Power PAC
body:
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/9?t=1&akid=39510%2E3422442%2Eoc9A5p)
You know I love oversight, Derek.
Let me tell you why. To me, oversight is about closing the gap between what the law says on paper and the difference it makes on the ground. The policy victories are meaningless if Americans don’t actually see the benefits in their day-to-day lives.
During Trump’s first term, we had a rare moment of bipartisan action when we passed a new law—signed by President Trump himself—that cracked down on surprise medical bills.
This issue is personal for me, because I was the victim of a surprise bill when my appendix burst. I received care at an in-network hospital, but my surgeon was out-of-network, leaving me on the hook for thousands of dollars.
I was proud to help pass this legislation to improve our health care system.
Well, take a look at this headline from this week:
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ttp-em-20250309-3?refcode=em20250309-39510&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=39510_3422442_oc9A5p&akid=39510%2E3422442%2Eoc9A5p)
Even when we can find common ground, we can’t count on Trump to actually get the policy done. When we look at his actions, rather than his rhetoric, it’s clear that he doesn’t actually care about getting families the help they need. We need Congress to step up.
**[So, if you can, would you chip in to Truth to Power to help us grow the number of elected officials who share my commitment to oversight and who will keep their promises and deliver for families?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ttp-em-20250309-3?refcode=em20250309-39510&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=39510_3422442_oc9A5p&akid=39510%2E3422442%2Eoc9A5p)**
Thanks for reading,
Katie

Former U.S. Congresswoman Katie Porter founded Truth to Power to support the campaigns of her fellow Democrats. Truth to Power supports candidates who will stand up to Donald Trump, fight to lower costs, and protect the environment. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/truth-to-power-1?refcode=em20250309-39510&amounts=&t=2&refcode2=39510_3422442_oc9A5p&akid=39510%2E3422442%2Eoc9A5p)**
---
Paid for by Truth to Power PAC
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you. But, if you'd like to receive fewer emails, [**you can do so here**](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/receive-fewer-ttp-emails/). If you need to remove yourself from our email list, [click here to unsubscribe](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/ttp-unsubscribe/?t=3&akid=39510%2E3422442%2Eoc9A5p).

To contribute via check, please address to: Truth to Power PAC, PO Box 5176, Irvine, CA 92616

Team,

I know how difficult it can be to keep up with Trump’s never-ending news cycle.

To constantly hear about the damage, and the pain and suffering, caused by Trump and his people.

To read about our country embarrassing itself, yet again, on the international stage.

And then to listen to the same tired reactions from our elected leaders who, like clockwork, express “ _concern_ ” about the insanity that is happening right before our eyes.

These are the people who _can_ actually DO something. These are the folks who have been _given power by the people_ to ACT in OUR best interest.

And I know that it can sometimes feel hopeless and frustrating. But—we also have power. **And we also must act to make our voices heard.**

I am running for Congress to rally the nation to our cause. Flipping this open seat sends a powerful message to every do-nothing member of Congress and every Democrat who is afraid to stand up and fight: **_we are fed up and DEMAND action._**

| We know there are a lot of demoralizing updates in the news right now. **But today we want to highlight one of our inspiring California Strong leaders!** California Attorney General Rob Bonta hasn’t been afraid to take the fight to Donald Trump. In fact, he is boldly taking the Trump Administration to court. **[Will you rush a donation today so CADEM can continue supporting our California Strong leaders who are standing up for Californians and defending us from the chaos-driven Trump Administration?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/103555448/532564286/803820036?refcode=20250309_FR_45DO_CAStrongBonta&amount=35&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzUvMS84MDk5NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0ODczYjFmMy00YmZkLWVmMTEtOTBjZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=AR0G4vWxeo_JMFpzbjoVJEqoYg9fuGsFqku7tzQsfbc=)** [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/103555449/532564287/803820036?refcode=20250309_FR_45DO_CAStrongBonta&amount=35&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzUvMS84MDk5NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0ODczYjFmMy00YmZkLWVmMTEtOTBjZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=AR0G4vWxeo_JMFpzbjoVJEqoYg9fuGsFqku7tzQsfbc=) We see it across the country, too many corporate and political leaders are shying away from taking on Donald Trump. But our California Strong leaders like Attorney General Bonta are showing the *COURAGE* and strength to stand up for us. Here are just some of the tangible actions that AG Bonta has taken: — In Trump’s first days in office, AG Bonta stood up for our Constitution by leading a coalition of states in **_filing a lawsuit against President Trump's executive order_** aimed at ending birthright citizenship; — **_AG Bonta has gone toe-to-toe with Elon Musk_** , challenging the authority of the DOGE team to unilaterally access sensitive Treasury Department data; — **_And he stood with dreamers_** by proactively defending their right to purchase healthcare through the Affordable Care Act. Leaders like Rob Bonta can stand up to Donald Trump knowing that the California Democratic Party and our grassroots movement will have his back. clean_body: We support California Strong leaders | = | We know there are a lot of demoralizing updates in the news right now. But today we want to highlight one of our inspiring California Strong leaders! California Attorney General Rob Bonta hasn’t been afraid to take the fight to Donald Trump. In fact, he is boldly taking the Trump Administration to court. = = We see it across the country, too many corporate and political leaders are shying away from taking on Donald Trump. But our California Strong leaders like Attorney General Bonta are showing the COURAGE and strength to stand up for us. Here are just some of the tangible actions that AG Bonta has taken: — In Trump’s first days in office, AG Bonta stood up for our Constitution by leading a coalition of states in filing a lawsuit against President Trump's executive order aimed at ending birthright citizenship; — AG Bonta has gone toe-to-toe with Elon Musk , challenging the authority of the DOGE team to unilaterally access sensitive Treasury Department data; — And he stood with dreamers by proactively defending their right to purchase healthcare through the Affordable Care Act. Leaders like Rob Bonta can stand up to Donald Trump knowing that the California Democratic Party and our grassroots movement will have his back. That’s why we are reaching out today and asking: = = = = = = Thank you, \- CADEM | | = Our Democratic leaders are working hard to make California a better place to live. From fighting for good-paying jobs, combating climate change, to expanding healthcare access so all Californians can lead safe, healthy, prosperous lives — California’s Democratic leaders turn progressive ideas into meaningful action. ---
Lots has happened over the past few days - let’s jump in!
### UPDATE: Our Open Letter to DOGE
We were thrilled by the overwhelming response to our open letter to DOGE! In the letter, we urged DOGE to champion the cause of reviewing our nation’s voter rolls. To gauge public opinion, we launched a poll alongside the letter on Truth Social, X, and Facebook—and the results were astounding: 98.8% support! WOW!
| [| | | True the Vote @TrueTheVote |
---|---|---
As promised, we are amping up our call to DOGE to help audit America’s voter rolls. Our country has several key elections coming up in 2025 - and a massive number of elections in 2026 (36 Governor’s races!). The time to have this conversation is ... right now.
What follows is an... x.com/i/web/status/1...

12:02 AM • Mar 6, 2025

**1.84K** Likes **1.25K** Retweets
**140 Replies**](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.bYZLwP2tKNW52PF5H5Fs6d632pOaHlQVWOb7P_0r4ZEDAluCEpWa0oh_813hbgkd0MW60uWMrAE4v7pUCH9cPhUX3QGzY1GhekjRTtg7d11FClrOlR-GyrEwKjKGQ7jJW7arKrZd09WOotbOP_LD_JRmlxsXDQxSKpdH3qVfu_VlxQ-TxT7CFeDeT7ClUBiweYpVQcn59wJL5uiogV5va61FYj10kXhWNpWjGPKwaAsKD8dhP_PLKmi7bF2SzpXGah3jQ9Nk8OUb_U2vumO2E8uC42YGmXp0RTGKnD4gdekRigKVTmfRozOvMke472Rm/4eo/lbx_cPZXTRS823EIUuyQHA/h2/h001.659UB5l3qtY8p0Lqkw9tMH19_abzkm2v-HUjyjGDfso)
Along with this incredible feedback, we also received important questions about how DOGE, as a federal department, could legally participate in a voter roll audit, given that voter rolls are managed at the state level. Many asked whether such an effort would even be constitutional. Let’s discuss ...
#### What is the Federal Government’s Role in Elections, Specifically Voter Rolls?
The federal government has limited but important oversight responsibilities concerning the accuracy of state voter rolls, primarily through the **[National Voter Registration Act (NVRA) of 1993](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.UJcOIEsxQ3qr5lEWJDDC4tdTK17Xul1vQH4JFxxIo7ATJSJA47aTfIVQksARFRCD3huJzcJrI7n9qvuzsNIgjycglNIsg4A1b7PF0I0WarYwXv2KSc22yaNVimZES8mpSLHwpviu3B96YkQIke4OkyiQwmuLMcfKw8J7VkTFjdkx4yyHS-314vhoNpFaw0iFvjoBGiAcwv0m5KU0wiFScFZMssIZzyfS7miEKmQA1rmb026j7NJ-DQx32JCdpYmUiijNIt_xtlTYuP_fHuswyQG3F6QX9W-7JwNN6yOubFnXqYoqUqge134unAt-jNo1/4eo/lbx_cPZXTRS823EIUuyQHA/h3/h001.Y1bwLHUEJcnfe9e69d1QFm2h2E8DERtPOYLPz-yXkJo)**. The **NVRA** is the main federal law governing voter registration and list maintenance. It sets specific requirements for how states must maintain voter rolls. The **DOJ** can file lawsuits against states that do not follow NVRA requirements, ensuring voter rolls remain accurate and up to date.
#### **Does the federal government directly audit voter rolls? No.**
No, the federal government does **not** directly audit state voter rolls. However, through the **DOJ** , it can investigate and take legal action if states fail to comply with **NVRA** requirements. Additionally, organizations and individuals can file lawsuits if they believe states are not properly maintaining their voter rolls.
#### **Are we recommending DOGE and/or the federal government assume control of state voter rolls? No!**
We are specifically advocating for DOGE to assist in allowing key federal datasets to be queried to resolve identity, residency, and citizenship in voter registration records - and making all findings public.
Through our IV3 project, we aggregate voter data directly from states. The arduous process of collecting and normalizing state data is done. We are ready to go. We do use other datasets, like the USPS National Change of Address registry and other residency-related refinement tools, and have limited access to a Social Security database that tracks mortality. But the best source for comprehensive data is the federal government. We would only need a limited query for verification purposes, which can be easily achieved.
If DOGE (or the DOJ) attempts to engage directly with the states, they should first reflect on what happened in 2017 to the President’s Advisory Committee on Election Integrity. [It didn’t go well.](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.UJcOIEsxQ3qr5lEWJDDC4g-n8_qhu8CMU6PofLEuQZHMfO9B6swsq4xYMrHEpyh2Rkw4LScOcqNLD_rKSl7vf4jRlCwNoLT1yNfSU9ubgpJAtgdBEIusxqghoV2nVwckmY7pfx3bjKSjRFLOpEACMg_iNobmn44qcUALXFJDToaZFfsg-woJkW80_dcHeuQ0bN6gLjEnJ3wlz5Jg7gfSwd_y4qBYz5WnAwP9JBwZ1omqK7TFrliPqHKqr4ZuRWrxz2Lrpt-Kn7HtA53s-bjqWci8qw5vyM73CCya4UznbaFda_Jhx-arj-wbteH_ndYcBy7OTdDoODq9B7fNsHdW5-tcIEl51n3jwRG2Pv25x-I/4eo/lbx_cPZXTRS823EIUuyQHA/h4/h001.FQzPd5gOiWwYXsaWdq4kOzwJmaKs05pepV29BGY4Tzs) States refused to participate, lawsuits started flying, and after less than a year, the Committee was dissolved.
And remember, states still can’t get the data they need from Dept of Homeland Security to determine a registered voter’s citizenship status. [Twenty-one states recently wrote to DHS](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.Uwn4nsck1coHXAZMOiMvuZ0-pPL4nvDlaP7znfPwSE7VSexuEOfCOzOcWBeM2M34QpJjxYGK2iC3DxLKOHLkKVHfshjp6GEdEfaB-Ik1kuqcCqDWhgrQvaIhCM7bb5cOXoh4W9mEeGvEDDpXqdoVODH2Uq5kIn28Xrm7bfIOT6xxaRXRHYYIZJp33bhiT8nQp4ZXPUdSMqziozwJWvf8G-fifOjRdKrFLmkP-A7HWfK-7dRgOK5QXLUM8HWa8D5o2QCmtto6JOskwQnBiSHhQ_gXGPfgFObNNmBj8OqwSskaS6-StkcXEJbjNh2_1c02Ri7NyPjRf8b8ZP3adaJETiRDIeTM4W4rzhf6POtOgY6qHByv-BH_uXXSg6Su5MQX/4eo/lbx_cPZXTRS823EIUuyQHA/h5/h001.o63nXEODrn_5-xpuEEsXXSi053n6jey5AxSBptxamjE), requesting expanded access, but their request as written only aims to band-aid a broken process with the archaic S.A.V.E. database. It’s not good enough. As we made clear in[ The 611 Project](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.bYZLwP2tKNW52PF5H5Fs6XnSdcrSmzbZQfStcjy751i-Ux7pGZZnGS3_QkKO6_ELjC5yHL05Cxwb3cle8YQ6yKtVkcypgOA0jUcNT7veafcBt32r4VXADhckHug6UWEKhYIR7sg19nlnmZvzM5_hcQc03WeL1Kb30CvJSu9cqXJttTHiZJ3qkJzu_QwDkPbAQslkgICyKcGa16pEsPAhhfD8YYUuNi69CFt8t0XzLhQP8whaA4I8qTLqbX31P6ScfKyZFCrQzPUzDChcv4T0qw/4eo/lbx_cPZXTRS823EIUuyQHA/h6/h001.r-CJf_pFrcwTmDvch3NewZls9APIVHtpYv3dF0rQAgg), there are specific actions states must take if they’re serious about access. Maybe the states’ letter is just the opening salvo, but ... we’re burning daylight.
Time is short. Our voter rolls are inaccurate. And it matters. [Take a look at this report True the Vote did after the Georgia general election in 2020. ](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.Grs4UHqmlwZE0ZbdGcvXvHutMki2dlrMZQTouCVCbRP2JLUQz2eicIFWi7yzjS5f7bsLoy0w02HSRbU5AZUZ3rb67SD2ZsMUkrupXwdToeWUPQx-KmPsZVcGYOwQpUKzvxl2dqN1lLjz_s4k2O1P98mk07lRaNQGjFf_OAPOlKeedbqsQH6d0KndjKSTzxTx-MZFpRh4Se62FXafoExjtcRzAGIfcedH8DtAYYO0UquiIhiCoa6V5ckrFsLZ0C59nmBfUo7Z6oeSf7kRP8ihoYwaRM3Q5x2MSJ8vHVmGdJYXtvkhCxY9g0E15LbO4QVU/4eo/lbx_cPZXTRS823EIUuyQHA/h7/h001.avqH8kS2wwKHmEYFpEkxvvIEssHBYLIHJwSc-z9RXMc)
**Are ineligible voters somehow still managing to cast votes? Yes. Is the volume significant enough to swing elections? Absolutely, yes.**
**Now is the time to bring common sense to bear and clean up the voter rolls.**
### On to Wisconsin!
There are critical elections and decisions of national significance on the ballot in Wisconsin’s April 1st election.
Specifically, Voter ID will be voted on as a state constitutional amendment, and there is a Supreme Court race in which one of the judicial candidates favors removing voter ID and has promised to strike down the state’s congressional map, costing Republicans two seats and potentially changing the balance of power in the House.
| [](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.UJcOIEsxQ3qr5lEWJDDC4jGNXUbPuFpxSdjQu0Ru-moPUzUx4iTo849irHLMxz-UeNqXyppVeD4VTD98th90AWCqMT3kLo_g8_LufPO5ePeXYb9BlXQioGoMFX7ao3i4I2VrF--wkwQGPTkOpHlmCCOwUfiKhE5-o9o4Cc0JcpygU2_gz-MyKpageAtED5KAuwsf81N2cndKq5aabBNs4PNW92MTXcMuFokWZvwTI81ugaXn5IXktL1oSgJPBkVWpk7I7H_JcuINQJFBsJX4Xg/4eo/lbx_cPZXTRS823EIUuyQHA/h8/h001.DcxVaeYwPnsT5f86IVUXUmSlLXJlPABy1mjcbHjS5Fw)
---
True the Vote has fought for years to help assure parity of party at the polls in Wisconsin so that both Left and Right have a balanced presence. Now is the time to put this to the test. If you live in Wisconsin and can work on Election Day, we are hosting training throughout the month, along with ballot drop box monitoring and support for election integrity defenders across the state. For more information, please email us at [wisconsin@truethevote.org](mailto:wisconsin@truethevote.org)
### ICYMI
Lastly, we’ve just received this 👇 . The official notice by the Georgia State Election Board of its dismissal of its lawsuit against us. Thank you to the Georgia SEB members who voted, unanimously, to drop the case.
|
---
This was a wild one. Lots we could discuss here, but the bottom line is we are glad the suit has been dismissed. Now, the question of what we should do to address the three years of lost time and resources we spent fighting a political smear campaign, well, that’s still TBD.
### The Week Ahead
We are in the final prep for re-opening of [IV3](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.ZOvxkEhKDw9DETb6Ummm9zQuo-zt7v1AcpXkOLMbvNt6YrZM562R3uGahNjwIn7jkGt_IWnw-uSgtquK_mdpjjdISa-tLImUGCjFLnGJ8wjT3VmRXSPspEOE6OIi5J1UV1LzPC7tMdsVp4bnzYDsd8r0V90WfWlWtG1bGMk40syykDXcfKTuDw8mQo83Hb8Dt0IVGK2aeZrmHMHrG9_puLqvTa668axURrEsr0V2WAxC8O49dlD7ndkEI941kuJd/4eo/lbx_cPZXTRS823EIUuyQHA/h9/h001.FINQCt3tGNwOwmXjUQRqqdHiRr3VwaArgCfNOo0Hd-M) and building out a new app, Legitrack, that we plan to launch in April. So, on this week’s docket will be lots of dev work; hopefully, hear from DOGE; Wisconsin training and support; and possibly a trip to DC, where I look forward to discussing everything from voter rolls to Konnech. Exciting times. Thank you for shoring us up as we continue to move ahead. We could not do what we do without you.
| [](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.bYZLwP2tKNW52PF5H5Fs6UAj3E_p8cNGXIOwo9MdaP4uURHu2CF_xBMsTaXvArHujZCYcWDflXvAHZOdWogeY67YYVzOuRaIaHqb0EQF0i6ti96rrq3S5noXyuTduKaOAwHSi5GlxhMe7SgIuMAOU1hhBsMnBq0rMgKGl_16DjLwnvi7feuTc7RXdIlytulegnMygykzmWv2TkjrZReLqDzmMLriZFyeNOgTfzggvYoNZiIbHOLam5mRLbta8U90pl13JVMPYVjQQcYgJws1jA/4eo/lbx_cPZXTRS823EIUuyQHA/h10/h001.qNEjPAEoyXP2Y785wBS1JAioie0iHM0GvibKXVKDiTc)

clean_body: Lots of great news to report! | | | March 09, 2025 | | | Hi Friend - Lots has happened over the past few days - let’s jump in! UPDATE: Our Open Letter to DOGE We were thrilled by the overwhelming response to our open letter to DOGE! In the letter, we urged DOGE to champion the cause of reviewing our nation’s voter rolls. To gauge public opinion, we launched a poll alongside the letter on Truth Social, X, and Facebook—and the results were astounding: 98.8% support! WOW! | Along with this incredible feedback, we also received important questions about how DOGE, as a federal department, could legally participate in a voter roll audit, given that voter rolls are managed at the state level. Many asked whether such an effort would even be constitutional. Let’s discuss ... What is the Federal Government’s Role in Elections, Specifically Voter Rolls? The federal government has limited but important oversight responsibilities concerning the accuracy of state voter rolls, primarily through the . The NVRA is the main federal law governing voter registration and list maintenance. It sets specific requirements for how states must maintain voter rolls. The DOJ can file lawsuits against states that do not follow NVRA requirements, ensuring voter rolls remain accurate and up to date. Does the federal government directly audit voter rolls? No. No, the federal government does not directly audit state voter rolls. However, through the DOJ , it can investigate and take legal action if states fail to comply with NVRA requirements. Additionally, organizations and individuals can file lawsuits if they believe states are not properly maintaining their voter rolls. Are we recommending DOGE and/or the federal government assume control of state voter rolls? No! We are specifically advocating for DOGE to assist in allowing key federal datasets to be queried to resolve identity, residency, and citizenship in voter registration records - and making all findings public. Through our IV3 project, we aggregate voter data directly from states. The arduous process of collecting and normalizing state data is done. We are ready to go. We do use other datasets, like the USPS National Change of Address registry and other residency-related refinement tools, and have limited access to a Social Security database that tracks mortality. But the best source for comprehensive data is the federal government. We would only need a limited query for verification purposes, which can be easily achieved. If DOGE (or the DOJ) attempts to engage directly with the states, they should first reflect on what happened in 2017 to the President’s Advisory Committee on Election Integrity. States refused to participate, lawsuits started flying, and after less than a year, the Committee was dissolved. And remember, states still can’t get the data they need from Dept of Homeland Security to determine a registered voter’s citizenship status. , requesting expanded access, but their request as written only aims to band-aid a broken process with the archaic S.A.V.E. database. It’s not good enough. As we made clear in, there are specific actions states must take if they’re serious about access. Maybe the states’ letter is just the opening salvo, but ... we’re burning daylight. Time is short. Our voter rolls are inaccurate. And it matters. Are ineligible voters somehow still managing to cast votes? Yes. Is the volume significant enough to swing elections? Absolutely, yes. Now is the time to bring common sense to bear and clean up the voter rolls. On to Wisconsin! There are critical elections and decisions of national significance on the ballot in Wisconsin’s April 1st election. Specifically, Voter ID will be voted on as a state constitutional amendment, and there is a Supreme Court race in which one of the judicial candidates favors removing voter ID and has promised to strike down the state’s congressional map, costing Republicans two seats and potentially changing the balance of power in the House. | True the Vote has fought for years to help assure parity of party at the polls in Wisconsin so that both Left and Right have a balanced presence. Now is the time to put this to the test. If you live in Wisconsin and can work on Election Day, we are hosting training throughout the month, along with ballot drop box monitoring and support for election integrity defenders across the state. For more information, please email us at mailto:wisconsin@truethevote.org ICYMI Lastly, we’ve just received this 👇 . The official notice by the Georgia State Election Board of its dismissal of its lawsuit against us. Thank you to the Georgia SEB members who voted, unanimously, to drop the case. | This was a wild one. Lots we could discuss here, but the bottom line is we are glad the suit has been dismissed. Now, the question of what we should do to address the three years of lost time and resources we spent fighting a political smear campaign, well, that’s still TBD. The Week Ahead We are in the final prep for re-opening of and building out a new app, Legitrack, that we plan to launch in April. So, on this week’s docket will be lots of dev work; hopefully, hear from DOGE; Wisconsin training and support; and possibly a trip to DC, where I look forward to discussing everything from voter rolls to Konnech. Exciting times. Thank you for shoring us up as we continue to move ahead. We could not do what we do without you. | cost of data and development, not to mention lawfare, is breathtakingly expensive, but we must press on. The work of election integrity is too important. Thank you for standing with us! We will never quit. Ephesians 6 FTW! Ever onward - | | | | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|--- | | Update your email preferences or unsubscribe (C) 2025 True the Vote 3120 Southwest Frwy Ste 101, PMB 19128 Houston, Texas 77098-4520, United States of America

clean_body: We want to hear from winners like you, Friend! | | | | FROM THE DESK OF THE VICE PRESIDENT: Friend, President Trump’s first address to Congress was historic! He’s achieved more wins in the first two months than any President in American History. While President Trump spoke of our progress, not a single Democrat stood, clapped, or even smiled through the address. Clearly President Trump is making the swamp creatures miserable. Speaker Johnson even had to THROW OUT Rep. Al Green for shouting and disorderly conduct. The disrespect for President Trump was infuriating, and the disrespect for America was disgusting. Clearly they don’t like us very much, but I’m more interested in your opinion. I’m hoping to read your answer by 11:59 PM TONIGHT. # **[Cross-examining Christ: Biden judge distorts Jesus to slam Trump — but at what cost?](http://trk.libertyledger.)**
---
WWJD: What would Jesus do?Based on a concept that St. Augustine developed — and then was popularized centuries later in the 1990s — WWJD became a topic of debate in a federal courtroom last month when U.S. District Court Judge Ana Reyes, a Biden appointee, asked Justice Department attorney Jason Lynch how Jesus Christ would respond to one of President Donald Trump's executive orders defending women from trans ideology.Reyes posed the bizarre question after reading aloud an email that she had received from a Christian who sought to evangelize her.
[**Read More**](http://trk.libertyledger.)

As we explore the best Celebrity Jeopardy! appearances, we go back to the times when celebrities exchanged their scripts for buzzers to show off their humor, intelligence, and comedic timing.

name: Brandon Gill
subject: Their lies won’t save them
body:
I just grilled the most corrupt mayor in the country. Here’s how it went:

| | | | [](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/a7jl/?__vbtrk=Mjc4OTUxOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjc4OTUxOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
---
Friend, I took the fight to **one of the most corrupt politicians in the country: Chicago Mayor Brandon Johnson.**
I went straight for the jugular because I promised the American people that I would stare down corruption without flinching... And that’s exactly what I did.
I asked him point-blank about _**the secret gift room in the Mayor’s office**_ ; an area kept under _lock and key_ that no member of the public is allowed to see. What is he hiding in there?
[STAND WITH REP. GILL: ROOT OUT CORRUPTION](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/a7jl/?__vbtrk=Mjc4OTUxOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjc4OTUxOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
We know from whistleblowers that he has received _**Gucci bags**_ , _**Hugo Boss cufflinks**_ , and other _**luxury gifts**_ that were showered on him by shadowy donors.
I pressed Mayor Johnson: **who are behind these handouts?**
Are they Criminal outfits? Cronies? And does he wonder if it’s suspicious that, while he allows millions of criminal illegal aliens to hide out in Chicago, he keeps receiving **_millions of dollars worth of luxury items from anonymous donors_**.
While Chicago crumbles under corrupt Democrat control their Mayor, Brandon Johnson, is playing dress-up with _**ill-gotten swag**_ , likely given to him by the **criminal cartels** , the **racketeers** his police force allows to operate, and **human traffickers** that have turned Chicago into a hub of cartel activity.
He can claim they were “gifts to the city” but we all know the truth. **His lies are so blatant that they are insulting.**
[STAND WITH REP. GILL: ROOT OUT CORRUPTION](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/a7jl/?__vbtrk=Mjc4OTUxOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjc4OTUxOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
Brandon Johnson must think we are fools. That’s why I want to hand it all to the feds and let the Department of Justice sort this out.
Friend, there is a reason you don’t see many Members of Congress fighting corruption like this... **They’re either captured by the forces of darkness or they’ve been part of them from the start.******
I’m in this fight for you and for every hardworking American sick of corrupt insiders _**_[but there is absolutely no way I can fight this way without your support](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/a7jl/?__vbtrk=Mjc4OTUxOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjc4OTUxOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)_**_**.**
The corruption in politics is so all-consuming that now that I have attacked one of the most corrupt politicians in the country the attacks against me are only going to get ten times worse.
So I’m asking you, if you care about the fight against corruption in our towns, our cities, and our government, to **_[please chip in to my fight to make sure I’m able to defend myself when they come back swinging for me](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/a7jl/?__vbtrk=Mjc4OTUxOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjc4OTUxOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)_.**
| | _Yours Sincerely,_
---
[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/a7jl/?__vbtrk=Mjc4OTUxOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjc4OTUxOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
**Congressman Brandon Gill**
[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/a7jl/?__vbtrk=Mjc4OTUxOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjc4OTUxOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)

[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/a7ji/?__vbtrk=Mjc4OTUxOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjc4OTUxOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
| | [](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/a7jj/?__vbtrk=Mjc4OTUxOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjc4OTUxOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)

subject: Elon Musk just revealed the shocking truth about democrat voter fraud…
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Troy Downing for Congress
body:
This is alarming.

| | | Peter,

Elon Musk just blew the lid off the Democrat's dirty secret: **they're exploiting Social Security, disability, and Medicaid to buy voters and rig our democracy.**

"It's a giant voter fraud scam," Elon said. They're importing voters to swing states to turn them blue, and if they succeed, they'll control the House, Senate, and presidency.

But Elon didn't back down. He stood up for what was right and supported President Trump because he knew the stakes.

**Now, they're hell-bent on destroying Elon for his bravery.** That's why we need your help to defend him and his groundbreaking work with DOGE, which is shedding light on the federal government's wasteful spending.

[**Will you stand with us and fight back against the Democrat's vicious attacks on Elon and DOGE?**](https://trk.emails.troydowning.)

name: The Flyover
subject: Armed Man Shot Near White House, Russian Spy Ring, and Jet Lightning
body:
Secret Service agents [shot and wounded](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kLFuwyAQQL_G3ojugBBnYOiSLV07WhSOcBUGC0ii_H2V7p2f9PT0vq7r1XHmcvt0G9kR6ypBHgUogbAGx_m16jnYSCRJzWTxpNEASljmZJHMCaIMpKXBQItzPqD79l4janWGme3bBgoBAbRaDtFECQ7RHCMRLHrSQIFHbYefymUkivlVH9QOvm5ztmmMvU_qY5KXSV52qnumN5rkpZNvNESn9mBPoqdaRxeubRTE5ooo5Jp4Jh4kUr13EuHeuNyEryW2WoYbXItANGdlUM3Nhv3JOXM3p0nDbXOc_yLGf1MeVv4GAAD__zdtZWQ) an “armed man” a block from the White House shortly after midnight Sunday while President Trump was away for the weekend.

| | |
---
| [](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx00LGO4yAQBuCnwZ2jYSDYLihOitzl2istAkM8JzCWTXKXt185u9pu2_lGM7_-P9fp6jjxcv_tMtkay4SA5xZUK2EKjtNr0k2wkQhJNWRlp6UBidA3s-0lmgG009oPQXnfaQfgh4EidjiY2LA9roGSIAG06k_RRAQnpTlHIui10ECBa9lOfwsvdaaYXuVJ28mX3CQ717oK9UvgKHC8lf_ulg4ROPLypL0eoMZHzdNeHpsnoS6UHSeB5hhmCvzIQl1Wx6Fd3VYX2vaZ1y_3Lq-O78t74xtlD606q-mzA4kGAFXXD6bZbFj_cUq8m05ouB-v3knrT809LX4EAAD__xSVbkM) | |
# Monday, March 10, 2025
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1913, William Knox bowled the first perfect 300 game—12 strikes in a row—ever recorded in competition at the American Bowling Congress Championships in Toledo. ([See Photos](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kLGOpDAQRL_GZEbttjEQOLiEbC-9EHmgPfjW0Mi2huPvTzP5ShWVSk-l9-dr_vIxxeP52-_kauAZATsJWiqYVx_TPZtmdYEISTfkVG-UBYUwNJsLpEghoDV-WMZgvRr7VQerH8tgl25sonvTQCtQAEYPbbABwStlu0AEgxEGaI2Vc_uX41E3CunmF-V24b1Jbqv1LEL_EjgJnK7raoNf6MH8_R4InConWnmLpXK-BU4nl1oETnzIusUiV3_LeLyjRqXlGfkgyvLBV6Isr5hS9Lv8PvjfpyuybiRDzKVKDSCffidJL8oCJ9Sqh27QVtkeO4MCpya79fxAiu2FgefuY_pcrz-pfDn8HwAA__-46nTL)) If this early Monday morning's got you all groggy, perhaps these lovely—and sometimes funny—[award-winning wildlife photos](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kL1u9CAQAJ8Gd1jLgn--guJr3F3alBY6FnsjbBAmh_z20aVPPdKMNJ-P9eE48rl9uINsDWlFwEGClgpW7zjeq-m8DURIuiOrJqNGUAhzt1vQA_4LfoBhJq2VnhVMM-FTgQ4BJt-xfdtAK1AARs99GAOCU2ocAhHMRhggzzWV_ivxWXcK8U4vKv0zHV20e635Evq_wEXg0lrrc8rXk99Y4ELni0s6DzqrwOV09buQzHuqaSsu77d0zRV_ycbRRw4k-XAbXRIBjcClK9bnxjHyNU7CwHY4jr_l-teJl8WfAAAA___gM2Bg) in our _Et Cetera_ section might provide just the lift you need. We're sponsored today by [BOXABL](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx00LGO4yAQBuCnwZ2jYSDYLihOitzl2istAkM8JzCWTXKXt185u9pu2_lGM7_-P9fp6jjxcv_tMtkay4SA5xZUK2EKjtNr0k2wkQhJNWRlp6UBidA3s-0lmgG009oPQXnfaQfgh4EidjiY2LA9roGSIAG06k_RRAQnpTlHIui10ECBa9lOfwsvdaaYXuVJ28mX3CQ717oK9UvgKHC8lf_ulg4ROPLypL0eoMZHzdNeHpsnoS6UHSeB5hhmCvzIQl1Wx6Fd3VYX2vaZ1y_3Lq-O78t74xtlD606q-mzA4kGAFXXD6bZbFj_cUq8m05ouB-v3knrT809LX4EAAD__xSVbkM), presenting an opportunity to invest in new groundbreaking building technology.
|
---
## Secret Service Shoots Armed Man
Secret Service agents [shot and wounded](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kLFuwyAQQL_G3ojugBBnYOiSLV07WhSOcBUGC0ii_H2V7p2f9PT0vq7r1XHmcvt0G9kR6ypBHgUogbAGx_m16jnYSCRJzWTxpNEASljmZJHMCaIMpKXBQItzPqD79l4janWGme3bBgoBAbRaDtFECQ7RHCMRLHrSQIFHbYefymUkivlVH9QOvm5ztmmMvU_qY5KXSV52qnumN5rkpZNvNESn9mBPoqdaRxeubRTE5ooo5Jp4Jh4kUr13EuHeuNyEryW2WoYbXItANGdlUM3Nhv3JOXM3p0nDbXOc_yLGf1MeVv4GAAD__zdtZWQ) an “armed man” a block from the White House shortly after midnight Sunday while President Trump was away for the weekend. The suspect, identified as 27-year-old Andrew Dawson of Indiana, [drove from his home state to Washington D.C.](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx00LFuwyAQxvGnwRvWcQbHGRi6eEvXjhaxj3AVhghI3Lx9le6dP-mnT_-vy3JxHDndPt1Otvm8IKCRMEgFy-Y4vhbdbdYTIQ0dWXXSagSFMHXBKofnjU7X6eoN0VkbXI1R2hinrkbjuWP71mBQoAD0MPV-9AhOqdF4Ipi00EAbt1z678ypBfLxlZ9U-jXvXbShtXsVw4fAWeB8HEfv80-io753gfOjCpwrrYWarFSevJKsIedW5e6SfEuJb6FJV3ba5JqTLzk11zgnmcgVeQRuJEN-VOqK3e4Hx8h1PAkNt91x_DvS_gvztPgbAAD__9eIaA4) on Saturday, according to a tip from local police who called him a “suicidal individual.” Secret Service found Dawson near his parked vehicle, and an “armed confrontation” took place after he “[brandished a firearm](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kDGP4yAQRn-N3RHNMBjjguKadLn2SovAEM-JmMiwifLvV9l-y0-f9PT0_l3WS5Ai--1vuLPvua4a9KSAFMKagpT3asbkM7NmGtnjbNACanDj5kkHdrikxURnr4sjh0iQMC4BZ4w0iv_QgBAQwJA7ZZs1BEQ7ZWZwZjDASXo9Tv-r7H3jXN71yccp1vtY_Nb7ow30Z9DnQZ_DY-dX-1yfcXSJhQd9blutXfabem3SWW31q7FqHA_uqvHxlMiKLJlpji4YylYHyBynKyXtrjgvkGg8fHq8pBRpdh4M3O5Byo9E_y3K0-vvAAAA__-Zt2H4),” according to a Secret Service statement. Dawson, who was also armed with a knife, was rushed to the hospital, and his condition is currently unknown.
## Syria Battles Leave 1,000 Dead
More than [1,000 people have been killed in Syria](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kL1uwzAQg5_G3hTc6dcZNHTJlq4djbN0itUqdiAJCfL2hbt3JAgS5Pd1na-US95un3Rn39M-S5BGgBIIc6Rc3rMeo0_MktXIHp1GCyhhGldPIZmF5JRQ06RIK3c2Z4UuLmdtFrRj9kcbKAQE0Go6JZskEKI1iRkmPWjgmPteT9973vrKqbz3J9dT2O9j8Wvvjzaoj0FeBnmhx8avdliHqD2HwoO8tHfNJKjQK3duonHoVDNt4ieX41kTYafWBbVGUay9CYsQIjNG46yMisPiTHAAaXEhmSTH6uPjdaSbdYOG251y-ZvU_0P09PI3AAD__xg7aFo) in the past few days in clashes between the newly installed government forces and loyalists of ousted President Bashar al-Assad. The violence began Thursday near Jableh, a longtime stronghold of Assad’s Alawite community, when loyalists ambushed government forces, triggering retaliatory attacks on Alawite neighborhoods. U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio [condemned the killings](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0z7GOhCAQgOGngW7NMCKwBcU1dnvtlQZhWOcOdaNE49tf9vqr_-RL_q_H8AhceHl-hpl8zeuAgN0N2puCIQUu16Bl8pkIqZXkldXKgEJwcvIYkk4mdM66fB91yjracTTBoqPo4l2yf2vQKlAAunVNNhkhKGW6TAROCw2UuK5b873yUifK5VoP2pq4zrL4qdbXLtoPgb3A_jzPZhzjuwnsFzp3gX3hgwT20ZG97PWTQFW5-fQ6uRTejRUannPg8ifW_w4Pj78BAAD__xHQUew), urging the interim Syrian government to protect civilians and hold the "radical Islamist terrorists, including foreign jihadis" accountable for the massacres.
## States Push for Permanent Daylight Saving
At least 20 U.S. states have passed legislation to permanently adopt Daylight Saving Time (DST), but [federal law prevents](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0z71u7CAQQOGngc7WMLD-KShu425vm9IiMNhE2Fh4YmvfPsr2aU_xSefjOT9dymlf_ruNLMcyI-CjAd0omINL-TUbGWwkQtKSrOqN6kAhDHK1PRowbvw00Xmvx-j7MZD3phvGEMKIMtlfDbQCBWD00MYuIjilukckgsEIAxQSl9p-lbTzSjG_ykW19WWT2a7Mxyn0P4GTwOm-75ar28-jVHacyt4u5RI4VVq-8zucAidOGzXOs6w2HHfKOZ1dLwwsm0v5DfNfo5fFnwAAAP__EhdWig) them from making the switch without congressional approval. Arizona and Hawaii are the only states that stay on standard time year-round. [This map shows the status of every state.](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kLtuhDAQAL_GdEbrNa8ULtLQXdqUaGWvYSPzEPaB7u-jS59-NCPN92N6kCTZ5i9a2ZW4TwjYarDawBRI0mtqquAiM7Kt2Jm-MR0YhKFaXGyJfe998DHajx4t-wE8hq4d2MTeV-LeNrAGDEBjhzp2EYGM6drIDEOjGuAgZT_rn122snBMr_3is_b7WiW3lHJkZT8VjgrH-77rS_KTkqdDCiXJ5Q0qHP2hcHxmnQsVzjr7kw4d6JVkXorOdMk2Z11kZe0X2mZWOFanC8ctKUnuetXAvJKkv3D5b8Tl8DcAAP__NSJiSQ) Although "The Sunshine Protection Act" has been introduced multiple times in Congress since 2018 to allow permanent DST, it has yet to pass.
* * *

([More](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0zzFvgzAQBeBfYzbQ-WwIGTx0YUvXjuiKz-FagyPbDcq_r-je7UlP-p7ex22-kUTZ7--0sashzQjYt2BaDbMnia_ZNt4FZmTTsNMXqwfQCGOzOhOu4wIceuwvfsGrsb0d0aOnkcH32Ig7NTAaNIA1YxeGgEBaD31ghtEqC-ylptx9JdnryiG-0pNzt6StiW6t9VGUeVM4KZyO4-iWz7LzUc5e4XRGhdM3bRSpXSlnKe1CUULKu1B7P6095bb8bBtnhVOTnX8cEqOU4aIs3DeS-LdW_3v_dPgbAAD__9WjXQA))
| Flying together with our sponsor [](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx00LGO4yAQBuCnwZ2jYSDYLihOitzl2istAkM8JzCWTXKXt185u9pu2_lGM7_-P9fp6jjxcv_tMtkay4SA5xZUK2EKjtNr0k2wkQhJNWRlp6UBidA3s-0lmgG009oPQXnfaQfgh4EidjiY2LA9roGSIAG06k_RRAQnpTlHIui10ECBa9lOfwsvdaaYXuVJ28mX3CQ717oK9UvgKHC8lf_ulg4ROPLypL0eoMZHzdNeHpsnoS6UHSeB5hhmCvzIQl1Wx6Fd3VYX2vaZ1y_3Lq-O78t74xtlD606q-mzA4kGAFXXD6bZbFj_cUq8m05ouB-v3knrT809LX4EAAD__xSVbkM) **The Assembly Line Comes to Home Construction** From clothes to cars to furniture, assembly lines revolutionized production -- making goods faster, cheaper, and most important of all, scalable. Yet the nearly $5T home construction industry remains stuck in the past. [BOXABL changes that for good.](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx00LGO4yAQBuCnwZ2jYSDYLihOitzl2istAkM8JzCWTXKXt185u9pu2_lGM7_-P9fp6jjxcv_tMtkay4SA5xZUK2EKjtNr0k2wkQhJNWRlp6UBidA3s-0lmgG009oPQXnfaQfgh4EidjiY2LA9roGSIAG06k_RRAQnpTlHIui10ECBa9lOfwsvdaaYXuVJ28mX3CQ717oK9UvgKHC8lf_ulg4ROPLypL0eoMZHzdNeHpsnoS6UHSeB5hhmCvzIQl1Wx6Fd3VYX2vaZ1y_3Lq-O78t74xtlD606q-mzA4kGAFXXD6bZbFj_cUq8m05ouB-v3knrT809LX4EAAD__xSVbkM) Their factory-built homes come off the assembly line in nearly four hours, fully equipped with plumbing, electrical, and HVAC. With 600+ already built and [**190,000+ reservations from potential buyers***](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx00LGO4yAQBuCnwZ2jYSDYLihOitzl2istAkM8JzCWTXKXt185u9pu2_lGM7_-P9fp6jjxcv_tMtkay4SA5xZUK2EKjtNr0k2wkQhJNWRlp6UBidA3s-0lmgG009oPQXnfaQfgh4EidjiY2LA9roGSIAG06k_RRAQnpTlHIui10ECBa9lOfwsvdaaYXuVJ28mX3CQ717oK9UvgKHC8lf_ulg4ROPLypL0eoMZHzdNeHpsnoS6UHSeB5hhmCvzIQl1Wx6Fd3VYX2vaZ1y_3Lq-O78t74xtlD606q-mzA4kGAFXXD6bZbFj_cUq8m05ouB-v3knrT809LX4EAAD__xSVbkM), demand is clear. One of the top U.S. homebuilders is even partnering with BOXABL. clean_body: Secret Service agents an “armed man” a block from the White House shortly after midnight Sunday while President Trump was away for the weekend. | | | | | | Monday, March 10, 2025 ---|--- Good Morning! On this day in 1913, William Knox bowled the first perfect 300 game—12 strikes in a row—ever recorded in competition at the American Bowling Congress Championships in Toledo. () If this early Monday morning's got you all groggy, perhaps these lovely—and sometimes funny— in our Et Cetera section might provide just the lift you need. We're sponsored today by , presenting an opportunity to invest in new groundbreaking building technology. | Secret Service Shoots Armed Man Secret Service agents an “armed man” a block from the White House shortly after midnight Sunday while President Trump was away for the weekend. The suspect, identified as 27-year-old Andrew Dawson of Indiana, on Saturday, according to a tip from local police who called him a “suicidal individual.” Secret Service found Dawson near his parked vehicle, and an “armed confrontation” took place after he “,” according to a Secret Service statement. Dawson, who was also armed with a knife, was rushed to the hospital, and his condition is currently unknown. Syria Battles Leave 1,000 Dead More than in the past few days in clashes between the newly installed government forces and loyalists of ousted President Bashar al-Assad. The violence began Thursday near Jableh, a longtime stronghold of Assad’s Alawite community, when loyalists ambushed government forces, triggering retaliatory attacks on Alawite neighborhoods. U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio , urging the interim Syrian government to protect civilians and hold the "radical Islamist terrorists, including foreign jihadis" accountable for the massacres. States Push for Permanent Daylight Saving At least 20 U.S. states have passed legislation to permanently adopt Daylight Saving Time (DST), but them from making the switch without congressional approval. Arizona and Hawaii are the only states that stay on standard time year-round. Although "The Sunshine Protection Act" has been introduced multiple times in Congress since 2018 to allow permanent DST, it has yet to pass. | | | | Dear friend, The 2026 midterms are closer than they seem, and preparation starts now. This election could be our turning point to weaken Trump’s MAGA agenda and take bold steps toward real progress in this country. Trump may still occupy the White House until 2028, but in 2026, we can disrupt his plans, win back key seats, and stop the chaos he and his allies have unleashed. Every seat we flip brings us closer to restoring accountability, justice, and opportunity for everyone in America. That’s why we’re calling on our grassroots team—on you—to help us build a winning coalition. We’ve set an ambitious goal to fund voter outreach and organize in battleground districts across the country. [**_We’re asking for $26 for 2026—or whatever you can give—to fuel this effort. Every dollar counts as we work to protect democracy and push back against MAGA extremism. Let’s show MAGA that their days of chaos are numbered. Together, we can make 2026 a turning point for our country._**](https://click.nodemleftbehind.com/click/gpnv-2wqafc-vijoje-kyd76rh2/) In Solidarity, The No Dem Left Behind Team

subject: Deadly Storms, State Bitcoin Reserve, and a 91-Run Softball Game
body:
Dallas area high schools Richardson Lake Highlands and Irving MacArthur scored 91 combined runs in a softball game last Tuesday, setting the record for the third-highest score in history.

# Monday, March 10, 2025
---|---
**Good morning!** On this day in 1890, the [John Sealy Training School for Nurses](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0z7GurCAQxvGngU4zjIMuBcVt7Pa2pzQgo5KgbJSs69ufuP2p_tX3S76f5_B0McVt_u9WtuUz5QEBdQVNpWAILqZrIBmsh2CUZKs6Ui2QBpKLbaDj4ByRd6OGBoxG0pr9g70HGFlGe2PQKFCgO6T6YQDQeNNOjCOBEgQcYsl7XRYu_HHHlK785r0e8yqTXUp5HaL5J7AX2J_nWZdjcXlLceM677PA_ruqgrsqf90R2PNW9rsajdxteJ0xpXi0nSCYVxfTFy9_vn1b_A0AAP__M0ZWfQ) opened with 18 students in Galveston's newly established John Sealy Hospital, becoming the first formal nursing school in Texas. If you hate changing your clocks twice a year, you'll be happy to know a Houston lawmaker has filed a bill to [end the practice](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kDGPszAMQH9N2IIcJ0AZMnwLW7_1RhSIgegCqYgL7b8_0Ztv8fKeni1_3fu7CzFs83-3kuXXlHoErCRoqaD3LsR3bwpvB_CtKsiqxqgaTAWmWOwNcEI_aGh1RQ01XrV-JG1q7YYGx7YI9oqBVqCgatCUtxYA26GtJ8LRgBIGyAdOe8kLMb1cnuI7HbSXY1qLaBfmRxb6n8BOYHeeZznGMH7jkp6Z03ZZAruNziywi2l0UWB37RTYgb5G88vlJy4zbY5JDiFGeaZn9DISf9iW5ZGYZNrkuRAvtMvMl5uGTPtBWXr3jmFeWGZ3hG2WHFYS2BW79Y8zxBhy3QgD8-pC_JzPf_7zsPgTAAD___cJePI) in Texas. Check out today's poll to weigh in on the issue. Today's sponsor is [Grounded Footwear](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0z7tqwzAUxvGnkbaYc44ujgcNhaIOJSktgXYzuiURleNgq3Hy9sXdu_--D_6fu37ncsmX094NydT7cewJSG1AbBD66HJ59JJH4yF2yJPBVqIGqUDys4kxpkBCKw-CBHZBeRFT6xQB6rB1PJv1DAQCgmpJNtsOgDrf6WOiIAGZhBRzHaemnlNNdzcfy2O8pakJ48CLOdd6nZl4YmQZ2WVZmmUK3_niS1kFI0svuEV8J83IHsT-Q8nXFQs7_3hk4vnwZd_4ZOJ1yaXkWbdMwmlw-W_P67_JN0O_AQAA___Ro1TX), the barefoot shoe that can help ease joint pain and pressure on the feet.

## Storm Rips Through Ellis County
Severe storms in Ellis County early Saturday [caused widespread damage](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx00LGOpDAMBuCnCZ1RYgIDRQoauj3pqiuRh5ghUoBRYobl7U-sdOVVLj7L8v__-Rq_KMSwvX7Ryk6-531EjTXoCowePYV4jbbw7ql9Zwp25mFNo22tbbE4jTVOVesbbJqKTNUy2dbXnqnGjqkqgruP6cpoo-sH2rLttMbu2TUz42S1UVazD7KnUhYW_qY8x2v_cCqnfS2iW0TeWVW9wkHhcJ5nuebtNoUDb3BkhcP7eMYwQaaZ5QLaPPDK6cXbFPj2hSnK8gP_diInucmAZ_KQ-cOJIixHEpBEk-zpniFyynC_I0fa2APNwgmy7GnNsAQBjjFkmPZjk0vhQAn63vQn0u8iOf8-w-3NQ1n9WinEn1Ty35o_Dv8GAAD__7DmgxA) that left one man dead and at least four people injured. Midlothian resident T.J. Bailey, 42, died when [his RV was flipped over](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kLGOgzAQRL_GdIvWiwlx4eIaulx7JTKxCdatcWRbIfz9ifTXzug9jebnNt1s4LA9vm30pr6XNBFSD9iBxMnZwMekGmdmdFo23shByQuqHlWzGo16tkg0d6ofBtTDvEjqB7c41aOW1yaYU4adRIn9QKq9akTSs74snu4KpVDoXagpt3X11b9tWfhIL5_be4oNm7XWZxHdl6BR0Ljve3ufy-b3cvaCxg8iaDwjQWMMjlNdg90g2g1-A7N3YAuUmnKEnJgL5BfYCh8SYqopP9m_YQ0FFhsDH1DsccqabNxzD8yhXAah8BFt4M-u-u9RL0N_AQAA__-7fWxX) near the Texas Motorplex drag strip in Ennis, where he had worked as a track specialist. His wife and two of his children were briefly hospitalized. Winds of up to 97 mph ripped off the storefront of an Ennis supermarket and the [roof of a nearby Days Inn hotel](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kLGO6yAURL8GOqzLNbZDQfEad3ntlhaGS4xEjAUoTv5-5fTbztGZkebnvtxtTHF__LdPMu0d8oKAg4BeSFi8jemzKO7NCl5LTkZOSo6gBlB8M84OAdygpjCNAVZvdVg1aCCwvbsF5NFcZdBLkDBMqLqbBkC96jEQOgWSKSAfWy5d26jR29aQPvlFpXP5yZPZWjsq6_8xnBnO53l2bq07nfXiDOevwnC-IoZzyTkI2vdYxZeILTdKosTjIC9yCKLSiwqJ2nK5fF6MP86YUqzjxBQ8njam73b784yXwd8AAAD__zpPYvM), where 20 people were staying at the time. Seven 18-wheelers were overturned on I-35E in Waxahachie. Golfball-sized hail fell throughout the area. Waxahachie City Council member Travis Smith shared a video on social media showing several downtown buildings with broken windows. [See photos here](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx00LFugzAQxvGnMduh88UGe_DQhS1dO6ID7GDJxJFxQ_L2Fdk7_08_nb6f63jlmOL99s2bd_UV8khIGvACEseFY3qPqlnchIuVjXeyV7JDpVE1q5PKGG11xzzTvFgVUKs-yCAnQ4RGN9GdGF4kStQ9qdZYRLKT7YKnWaEUCv0Say5tXX31L95DeuenL-2ctya5tdbHLi5fggZBw3Ec7RGYzyho4FLjnPwZPNfVF0HDPZe6wkeClWOCveaygU8p7jDn33t9w8Ip8Q4hlwrHeS9oINODDJ3R2hN0NhhQ0gYwMyFY3wVSk5HY901xy-OIJ9f1QuFt45g-z9Z_13s6-gsAAP__vkRu7g).
## Texas Bitcoin Reserve Proposal Clears Senate
The Texas Senate approved legislation to [create a strategic Bitcoin reserve](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kLHOozAQhJ_GdEZrY4xduLiGLtdeiRZYk9U5EBkHkrc_keK6vxvNjD6N5s9tuCEnXpff-KBQ3nEbNOhWQiMVDDNy-gymmsMIs1cVBdUZZcG0YKp7oLGxcaaRnLIdOiSHI0TypkXyDbQVhwsGjQIFbadN7TyA9qO3kfRkQAkDNHPZcl3uVOiNe0yf7aBcT9ujSuFeynMXzS-he6H78zzrv8eLrlDoHnPhKZHQ_UrnLnT_3BIXni75ZclEC-8JyyvTf2-nFQvJkVOSUyYsvC5y5DJtvMpMO-XjKmvrpZpi6xRZ6a0haWJ00qm5lTZi1NQ4D9pXOczPk1Pi3XbCwPJATt_15cc7j6D_BQAA__-JcXcC) last Thursday, the same day President Donald Trump signed an executive order establishing a national cryptocurrency reserve. [Senate Bill 21](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx00L1u6yAYxvGrgS3o5QV_MDCcKLLOkHRoho4WxuCg4mIZ6tp3XyV795-eR_p_3PqbCTF8TW9mdrrsPvUIWJ1AnDj0ownx6CUd9QCj4tRp3kheg6xA0of20FplOcfKKdUK2ZhBeu4Hq5y3VYM06OcYCA4cqgYlaxUAqkHV3qGVwIkEN4aSVlYerrjdZB-PtLmV2TTTqB-lLJmIfwQ7gp01SygpshdkU9oIducQ4zWlz--FYPc_5JLWg5m87ER0VzfdXc5EXFr1TrB-WiIu9zNyuupx-Qkxhlw3RMI0mxBfp-XPCpvG3wAAAP__jqtdrg) would establish a fund invested in crypto assets with a market capitalization of at least $500 billion over a 12-month period, a criterion that only Bitcoin currently meets. The fund would be managed by the Texas Comptroller with input from a five-member advisory committee. Proponents of the legislation say diversifying the state’s cash reserves would provide a hedge against inflation, while critics argue that the [volatility of cryptocurrency](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kLGOszAQhJ_GdEbrxUAoXPwNXf72SrTGS2Kdgy3bguTtT0lz1bUzmvmk7-u6XMkHv9_-04NNfW5xQcBeQicVLI58eC26ccaCm1TDRo1aDaB70M3dWIvbpWMEUtQ7y2unsFM9TZMewPZj4837DDoFCvoRdXuZAHCy07AxrhqU0MDO15jbeufKTypbeMWDc7vGRxPMvdZURPdP4CxwPs-z_X7S_i4FzjufReD8mckUg69-_Q0K71RZUko5Hlzkml-pRpm5cD5YpkC7wLnJxqXTh-DLMAoNtwf58IHXP20cBn8CAAD__y6rYxI) makes investing in the asset inherently risky. The bill advanced to the House after a 25-5 bipartisan vote. If enacted, Texas would become [one of the first states in the country](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kL2OqzAQhZ_GdEbjwcClcHEbumy7JfLPkFjrYISHEN5-Rfptphh9-s7R-b5NNxtTXO5f9kmG33OeELCV0EgFU7AxnZOugnEQBlWRUb1WHegWdPUwam47B64NapixUT32lhQocKSchjBU0VwyaK5n26Ou_w0AOLihmwm9BiU0UIict5ofxPS2ZU5nftFW-_ysknkwr0U0_wWOAsfjOGqf4xKo_FyAwHHNKfpT4HgFCRyhuU4ncPzYZNm3OxUZF7nLIgtbpiI360lyluvOct1dil7O-xIujLP027lyrjYT1iOmFEvXCw33p43p04r_nOll8DcAAP__9LBpgw) to establish a strategic Bitcoin reserve.
## 91-Run High School Softball Game
Dallas area high schools Richardson Lake Highlands and Irving MacArthur [scored 91 combined runs](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx00LGOrCAUxvGnwQ5zOCJKQXEbu7ntlgb1MJDgYIAdZ95-42y99S_558v3dZtvNsTwuP-3O5n6cmlGwJ5DxwXMmw3xPctmMwtsWjRkxCCFAtmDbLzpHShNHSroOhj14khoQQqGXm8jWdUEc8WgEyCgH1C2owZAvWjlCFcJgkmgLdSU2-qp0ssWF9_pSbld095E42s9Cuv-MZwYTud5tqvP6XEpw6kcKdfCcPLh7svqU4oMJ5trWCMxnD5BfiH_VV6Sq4uNkVeye-Fa8Pz9KBwBYexH1R7-aLLZjjPEGIoamIT7bkP8zKl__vM0-BMAAP__kKFnUA) in a softball game last Tuesday, setting the record for the third-highest combined score in high school softball history. Irving MacArthur scored 21 runs in the fourth inning but ultimately lost the 48-43 contest. Prior to the matchup, the Lady Cardinals had only scored 35 runs the entire season, recording only two runs in each of their two previous games. One team scored 10 or more runs in multiple innings, but the scoreboard could [only display single-digit scores](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kDFvwyAUhH8N3rDeexAcBoYu3tK1o4VtsGnBRIbGyb-vEqlSl043fLrT6fu4DBcbYtiWd5ucqXefBwI6cRAcYZhtiI9BNrMZYdbYOIOdRAXyBLJZjSJFYpJq6kCjVR6EP48zWg1IUghsgnmOgUBAOHUk27MGID1q5R1NEpBJcHOoeW_r6qq72-LjI9_c3k45NdGstV4LE2-Mekb9cRxtCU_EqF_DsvIyrTlHRv2r-5v8D-Ml-zraGHl1NhU-5TSGzXGfd66R799b4YCfW_qComlZmt3M1yPEGIrqmIQl2RBfb-q_em6GfgIAAP__7VxnPg). Together, the teams tallied a combined 44 hits, 30 errors, 48 walks, 9 strikeouts, 12 stolen bases, and 567 pitches. Lake Highlands pitcher Rhia McCarty played the entire 7-inning game, throwing 256 pitches. In a Friday matchup, MacArthur defeated Lake Highlands 25-24 after first falling behind 21-5 in the third inning. It was their [first win of the season](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kL2O4yAQgJ8Gd0TjMQ6moLjG3V57pTUBbNBhEwEbx29_ItU128yfRp--mT9fyxeFGI7tN-1O1_eaFgQcOQy8h8VSiNciOqsfYFXfOd1L0d9BjCA6ry1NBleQalAolFTGWLByWslIYew6dUE3GAw99DBKFLdJAaB6qPvq0AjomQBnQ035Vr2r7k1ljVd6uXwzae-i9rU-Cxt-MZwZzud53r4L1WTp8qW0HYZzqSlfLT9TroXh7MPmeTE-pdjGaa0Piq1sMgxnGFqYGM47GcrVf2e-UTgKz-7ljs1x-rSVR_rreMNFOmzh4eAfR_4fc0IYUU0DgGQ4d1nb5xliDOUumYBtpxA_t9Qfn_vS-C8AAP__sHN7vA).

Golfball-sized hail fell throughout the area. Waxahachie City Council member Travis Smith shared a video on social media showing several downtown buildings with broken windows. . Texas Bitcoin Reserve Proposal Clears Senate The Texas Senate approved legislation to last Thursday, the same day President Donald Trump signed an executive order establishing a national cryptocurrency reserve. would establish a fund invested in crypto assets with a market capitalization of at least $500 billion over a 12-month period, a criterion that only Bitcoin currently meets. The fund would be managed by the Texas Comptroller with input from a five-member advisory committee. Proponents of the legislation say diversifying the state’s cash reserves would provide a hedge against inflation, while critics argue that the makes investing in the asset inherently risky. The bill advanced to the House after a 25-5 bipartisan vote. If enacted, Texas would become to establish a strategic Bitcoin reserve. 91-Run High School Softball Game Dallas area high schools Richardson Lake Highlands and Irving MacArthur in a softball game last Tuesday, setting the record for the third-highest combined score in high school softball history. Lake Highlands pitcher Rhia McCarty played the entire 7-inning game, throwing 256 pitches. In a Friday matchup, MacArthur defeated Lake Highlands 25-24 after first falling behind 21-5 in the third inning. name: History Facts
subject: Why Queen Elizabeth I had black teeth
body:
You may think you have a sweet tooth, but it likely doesn’t compare to the sugar craze that swept through the English upper crust in the 16th century.
[](https://track.historyfacts.com/?xtl=1krv9cfc68dgcdeqsbdp3ttd18mlbzdrd2si50ck9r8eaysda4qyyf8lccr0jp5l4fy168ygocp9plnyb3m1auxqfv4aehdsct9p8md2o1aqsjld7dzzrpgguzkadycy6y49qi5u0oh5osnl4hrif5kxk&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sswmtj&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)

# [Queen Elizabeth I’s teeth turned black from too much sugar.](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sswmtj&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
### [Famous Figures](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sswmtj&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
[](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sswmtj&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
| Y ou may think you have a sweet tooth, but it likely doesn’t compare to the sugar craze that swept through the English upper crust in the 16th century. Due to colonial trade, sugar — a rare seasoning and preservative in the medieval period — flowed into Elizabethan England. Yet because of its high cost, the sucrose could only be purchased by the wealthy. The treat became the central ingredient of lavish banquets, and cookbooks of the well-to-do began to collect delectable dishes made with this new confection. However, sugar’s deleterious dental effects weren’t as well known at the time, and the most high-profile victim of this sweet ignorance was none other than Queen Elizabeth I herself.
---
It was no secret that the queen possessed [a legendary sweet tooth](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sswmtj&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com); historians say she even used a sugar-based toothpaste to polish her teeth, which 10 out of 10 dentists _don’t_ recommend. In 1599, a German traveler named Paul Hentzner described the 65-year-old queen and revealed what a lifelong sugar addiction can do: “Her Lips narrow and her Teeth black... a defect the English seem subject to, from their too great use of sugar.” Yet the queen’s black, rotting teeth didn’t detract from her appeal. Because only the rich could afford sugar (and the tooth decay it engendered), black teeth became seen as fashionable, and people would artificially blacken their teeth with soot to fit in, a fad that eventually faded after the reign of Elizabeth I.
[](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sswmtj&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) | [](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sswmtj&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)

## Queen Elizabeth I made red hair fashionable.
The Tudor dynasty, which ruled England from the coronation of Henry VII in 1485 until the death of Elizabeth I in 1603, was known for its redheads. Henry VIII, the most infamous of the Tudor kings, was described as having golden-red hair, but it was his daughter Elizabeth who really made fiery manes fashionable. A natural redhead, Elizabeth chose to forgo the use of wigs (which were fashionable at the time) in favor of her own red locks, and even dyed her horses’ tails the same color. There are [a few theories](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sswmtj&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) as to why her majesty chose this striking color, including that she wished to put questions about her legitimacy to rest by highlighting the hair color she shared with her father, the former king. Whatever the reason, the fashion trend stuck, and courtesans dyed their hair and beards to show their allegiance. The Elizabethan era was a golden age not just for England, but for redheads, too.

name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: Monday News Digest: 3/10/2025
body:
Colorado GOP Chair Race Heats Up – Debate Preview & Key Issues
| | | | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
---
**March 10, 2025** **MONDAY NEWS DIGEST** **By Heidi Ganahl**
| | Dear Readers,
The race for Colorado GOP Chair is heating up ahead of the March 29 election, and this Wednesday, the candidates will take the debate stage for the first time. This is a defining moment for the party’s future, and Rocky Mountain Voice is committed to keeping you informed every step of the way.
**Where the Race Stands**
With _five candidates_ now in the mix, we’ve been reaching out to each of them for their stance on the party’s most pressing issues. So far, [only Brita Horn has responded](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) to our questionnaire, providing insights into her vision for the party.
Here’s where the field stands so far:
✅ **[Brita Horn](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** – Former Routt County Treasurer, fire chief, and grassroots leader, focused on unity, voter engagement, and fundraising. You can read Brita’s answers to our questionnaire [here](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.).
✅ **[Darcy Schoening](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** – Current Colorado GOP Director of Special Initiatives, aligned with outgoing chair Dave Williams, running on a hardline conservative platform. ✅ **Lori Saine** – Weld County Commissioner and former State Representative, known for her strong stance on election integrity and conservative activism. Running with Hope Scheppelman as vice chair, who currently sits as vice chair under Dave Williams.
✅ **[Richard Holtorf](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** – Retired Army Colonel, former State House Whip, and Washington County GOP Chair, emphasizing party strategy and rural outreach. Running with Danielle Neuschwanger as vice chair and Nicole Brittain as secretary
✅ **Jimmy Mack** – Recently entered the race, details pending.****
**Key Issues at Stake**
The biggest debate among candidates is whether the party should [opt out of the primary](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) and return to a caucus-and-assembly system. This would mean that **Republican voters would no longer receive a primary ballot** , with nominees chosen by a select group of delegates instead. Some argue this strengthens party control, while others fear it will further alienate voters.
Another key topic is **fundraising**. With the party struggling financially, candidates must present a clear strategy to **regain donor trust** and ensure resources are available to support candidates in 2026.
**What’s Next?**
We will be sending our candidate questionnaire to Richard Holtorf and Jimmy Mack this week and will post an updated article as responses come in. As a reminder, no other candidates have responded to our request for answers to the questionnaire. If you are a voting member of the State GOP Central Committee, **now is the time to ask the tough questions** **and demand clear answers**.
Stay tuned for our debate coverage on Wednesday and check out our latest articles on each candidate to stay informed.
In liberty and truth,
Heidi
---
|
---

---

Unlike the federal government, the state cannot increase borrowing or mint additional currency._

**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _Meet Colorado’s special districts. Your wallet knows them better than you. Your tax dollars feed special districts most can’t even name. They siphon cash quietly until they are exposed._ __

It was not just a failed system; it was a weapon – an insidious mechanism that fostered and cultivated powerful lies about my self-image and self-worth that took decades to unlearn._ __
**[Walcher: Paying for what ought to be free](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _If I offered you a thousand dollars not to steal my car, would you be any less likely to steal it? What if I offered you a million? If you’re like most people, you would answer that you weren’t planning to steal it anyway. You’re not a thief so the discussion is pointless. Although if I were serious, you might take the money anyway._

clean_body: Colorado GOP Chair Race Heats Up – Debate Preview & Key Issues | | | | March 10, 2025 MONDAY NEWS DIGEST By Heidi Ganahl | | Dear Readers, The race for Colorado GOP Chair is heating up ahead of the March 29 election, and this Wednesday, the candidates will take the debate stage for the first time. This is a defining moment for the party’s future, and Rocky Mountain Voice is committed to keeping you informed every step of the way. Where the Race Stands With five candidates now in the mix, we’ve been reaching out to each of them for their stance on the party’s most pressing issues. So far, to our questionnaire, providing insights into her vision for the party. Here’s where the field stands so far: ✅ – Former Routt County Treasurer, fire chief, and grassroots leader, focused on unity, voter engagement, and fundraising. You can read Brita’s answers to our questionnaire . ✅ – Current Colorado GOP Director of Special Initiatives, aligned with outgoing chair Dave Williams, running on a hardline conservative platform. ✅ Lori Saine – Weld County Commissioner and former State Representative, known for her strong stance on election integrity and conservative activism. Running with Hope Scheppelman as vice chair, who currently sits as vice chair under Dave Williams. ✅ – Retired Army Colonel, former State House Whip, and Washington County GOP Chair, emphasizing party strategy and rural outreach. Running with Danielle Neuschwanger as vice chair and Nicole Brittain as secretary ✅ Jimmy Mack – Recently entered the race, details pending.** Key Issues at Stake The biggest debate among candidates is whether the party should and return to a caucus-and-assembly system. This would mean that Republican voters would no longer receive a primary ballot , with nominees chosen by a select group of delegates instead. Some argue this strengthens party control, while others fear it will further alienate voters. Another key topic is fundraising. With the party struggling financially, candidates must present a clear strategy to regain donor trust and ensure resources are available to support candidates in 2026. What’s Next? We will be sending our candidate questionnaire to Richard Holtorf and Jimmy Mack this week and will post an updated article as responses come in. As a reminder, no other candidates have responded to our request for answers to the questionnaire. If you are a voting member of the State GOP Central Committee, now is the time to ask the tough questions and demand clear answers. Stay tuned for our debate coverage on Wednesday and check out our latest articles on each candidate to stay informed. | [](https://substack.com/redirect/0c52ec3b-4a10-4e14-9f45-5bd1c279489b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|

Mississippi is known for having high car tag costs compared to other states, leaving many residents wondering why these fees are so significant. Car tags, or vehicle registration fees, are used for maintaining roadways, funding schools, and supporting county budgets, but the way these fees are calculated in Mississippi can make them particularly steep. Several factors contribute to the high cost, including vehicle valuation, tax structures, and the allocation of funds to various public services.
**Vehicle Value as a Primary Factor**
In Mississippi, one major reason car tags are expensive is that the registration fee is based on the assessed value of the vehicle. The state uses a percentage of the vehicle's "ad valorem" value, or fair market value as determined by the state, to decide how much residents must pay. Newer or more expensive cars will have higher registration costs, while older vehicles tend to have lower fees. This differs from some other states, where a flat fee is charged regardless of a car's value, making Mississippi's approach unique and sometimes costly. For example, a recent model car worth $30,000 will generate a significantly higher fee than an older car valued at $5,000.
**Property and Privilege Taxes Combined**
The Mississippi car tag cost includes both a property tax and a privilege tax. The property tax portion goes to the county where the vehicle is registered and helps support various public services, including roads and bridges, schools, and emergency services. The privilege tax is essentially a fee for the privilege of operating a vehicle on Mississippi roads. This combination means that vehicle owners in Mississippi pay a compounded cost when registering their cars, a structure that is designed to support infrastructure and community needs. However, it can feel burdensome because both taxes add up quickly, especially on higher-value vehicles.
**Support for Local Schools and County Budgets**
A significant portion of the revenue collected from car tags goes to fund local schools, which rely heavily on property tax income. In Mississippi, this revenue helps cover teacher salaries, school maintenance, and educational programs, but the largest part of these taxes fund school administration not direct classroom expenses. This reliance on car tags for school funding means that registration fees are mostly a local tax to support K-12 schools, which would otherwise rely entirely on property taxes levied on homes and land. Additionally, counties depend on car tag revenue to balance their budgets, fund road repairs, and maintain local infrastructure.
**Limitations on Tax Deductions**
Mississippi residents face an additional challenge in that these car tag fees are less flexible in terms of tax deductions than in some other states. Federal tax policies allow deductions for property taxes, which would include a portion of vehicle registration fees. However, this does not apply universally, and many Mississippi residents find that they cannot deduct the full cost of their car tags, increasing the financial strain of this expense.
**Efforts to Mitigate Costs**
The state has taken some measures to reduce the burden of high car tag costs. For example, Mississippi offers some discounts for veterans, senior citizens, and people with disabilities, and it allows partial rebates on vehicles that are sold or totaled before the tag expires. Nevertheless, these benefits apply only to specific groups and do not broadly reduce the costs for the majority of residents.
In conclusion, the majority of the money paid by Mississippi residents for car tags goes to local governments and this excessively high tax is the result of poor choices and liberal polices by local politicians.
